창세기 4:1.content: The man knew Eve his wife. She conceived, and gave birth to Cain, and said, “I have gotten a man with Yahweh’s help.”

창세기 4:2.content: Again she gave birth, to Cain’s brother Abel. Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.

창세기 4:3.content: As time passed, Cain brought an offering to Yahweh from the fruit of the ground.

창세기 4:4.content: Abel also brought some of the firstborn of his flock and of its fat. Yahweh respected Abel and his offering,

창세기 4:5.content: but he didn’t respect Cain and his offering. Cain was very angry, and the expression on his face fell.

창세기 4:6.content: Yahweh said to Cain, “Why are you angry? Why has the expression of your face fallen?

창세기 4:7.content: If you do well, won’t it be lifted up? If you don’t do well, sin crouches at the door. Its desire is for you, but you are to rule over it.”

창세기 4:8.content: Cain said to Abel, his brother, “Let’s go into the field.” While they were in the field, Cain rose up against Abel, his brother, and killed him.

창세기 4:9.content: Yahweh said to Cain, “Where is Abel, your brother?” He said, “I don’t know. Am I my brother’s keeper?”

창세기 4:10.content: Yahweh said, “What have you done? The voice of your brother’s blood cries to me from the ground.

창세기 4:11.content: Now you are cursed because of the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.

창세기 4:12.content: From now on, when you till the ground, it won’t yield its strength to you. You will be a fugitive and a wanderer in the earth.”

창세기 4:13.content: Cain said to Yahweh, “My punishment is greater than I can bear.

창세기 4:14.content: Behold, you have driven me out today from the surface of the ground. I will be hidden from your face, and I will be a fugitive and a wanderer in the earth. Whoever finds me will kill me.”

창세기 4:15.content: Yahweh said to him, “Therefore whoever slays Cain, vengeance will be taken on him sevenfold.” Yahweh appointed a sign for Cain, so that anyone finding him would not strike him.

창세기 4:16.content: Cain left Yahweh’s presence, and lived in the land of Nod, east of Eden.

창세기 4:17.content: Cain knew his wife. She conceived, and gave birth to Enoch. He built a city, and named the city after the name of his son, Enoch.

창세기 4:18.content: Irad was born to Enoch. Irad became the father of Mehujael. Mehujael became the father of Methushael. Methushael became the father of Lamech.

창세기 4:19.content: Lamech took two wives: the name of the first one was Adah, and the name of the second one was Zillah.

창세기 4:20.content: Adah gave birth to Jabal, who was the father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.

창세기 4:21.content: His brother’s name was Jubal, who was the father of all who handle the harp and pipe.

창세기 4:22.content: Zillah also gave birth to Tubal Cain, the forger of every cutting instrument of bronze and iron. Tubal Cain’s sister was Naamah.

창세기 4:23.content: Lamech said to his wives, “Adah and Zillah, hear my voice. You wives of Lamech, listen to my speech, for I have slain a man for wounding me, a young man for bruising me.

창세기 4:24.content: If Cain will be avenged seven times, truly Lamech seventy-seven times.”

창세기 4:25.content: Adam knew his wife again. She gave birth to a son, and named him Seth, saying, “for God has given me another child instead of Abel, for Cain killed him.”

창세기 4:26.content: A son was also born to Seth, and he named him Enosh. At that time men began to call on Yahweh’s name.
